title: lonchocarpus subglaucescens
intro: Lonchocarpus subglaucescens, also known as the "pangolin tree," is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Lonchocarpus subglaucescens include its potential to help alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and pneumonia, as well as its possible antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made from its leaves, bark, or roots, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or decoctions.
